executive_summary.question: If a person intends to kill one individual but instead unintentionally causes the death of a different person, how is the resulting culpable homicide classified?
executive_summary.generated_answer: If a person intends to kill one individual but instead unintentionally causes the death of a different person, the resulting culpable homicide is classified as culpable homicide by causing death of person other than person whose death was intended [Chunk-ID: cb540380-0fc6-40ad-bf08-4db5f0ea2a0a]. According to section 102, the culpable homicide committed by the offender is of the description of which it would have been if he had caused the death of the person whose death he intended or knew himself to be likely to cause [Chunk-ID: cb540380-0fc6-40ad-bf08-4db5f0ea2a0a]. This means that the offender's intention or knowledge regarding the original intended victim is used to determine the classification of the culpable homicide, even though the actual victim was different [Chunk-ID: cb540380-0fc6-40ad-bf08-4db5f0ea2a0a].

evidence_analysis.claim_text: The culpable homicide committed by the offender is of the description of which it would have been if he had caused the death of the person whose death he intended or knew himself to be likely to cause.
evidence_analysis.supporting_evidence:
Whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death,
or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the
knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death, commits the offence of culpable
homicide.

evidence_analysis.claim_text: The offender's intention or knowledge regarding the original intended victim is used to determine the classification of the culpable homicide.
evidence_analysis.supporting_evidence:
Here, although A was doing an unlawful act, he was not
guilty of culpable homicide, as he did not intend to kill B, or to cause death by doing an act
that he knew was likely to cause death.
